title: pelargonium laevigatum subsp. diversifolium
intro: Pelargonium laevigatum subsp. diversifolium, also known as the Cape Geranium, is a species of flowering plant in the Geraniaceae family, native to the southwestern coast of South Africa. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is used to treat a variety of health issues, such as digestive problems and sore throats. The health benefits of Pelargonium laevigatum subsp. diversifolium include reducing fever, alleviating coughs and colds, and soothing digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy. Herbal preparations of Pelargonium laevigatum subsp. diversifolium include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
